data.title: Comprehensive Healthcare Systems upsizes offering to $5.6M with new units priced at a premium
Sanmina targets $16B revenue in 2027 with AI-driven growth and ZT Systems integration
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sanmina Corporation (SANM) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Jure Sola, Co-Founder, Executive Chairman & CEO, opened the call stating he is "very pleased with our performance for the first quarter," highlighting revenue of $3.19 billion, a non-GAAP operating margin of 6%, non-GAAP diluted earnings per share of $2.38, and cash flow from operations of $179 million.
  • Sola emphasized, "I'm excited about the future opportunities that we have in front of us."
  • Sola indicated strong performance in communication networks, cloud, and AI infrastructure, as well as the successful integration of ZT Systems, citing ZT revenue at $1.964 billion for the quarter.
  • "Fiscal year '26 is on tracking to our expectation, the way I would say it, great start to fiscal year 2026," said Sola.
  • Jonathan Faust, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "We're very pleased with our first quarter results, which as you can see, either met or exceeded all of our outlook commitments."
  • Faust added, "Both the core Sanmina business and the ZT Systems business came in near the high end of their respective outlook ranges."

Outlook

  • For Q2 2026, Sanmina expects revenue between $3.1 billion to $3.4 billion, with the midpoint of $3.25 billion reflecting 62% growth compared to the same period a year ago.
  • Non-GAAP operating margin guidance is 5.7% to 6.2%, with non-GAAP diluted EPS projected at $2.25 to $2.55, based on approximately 56 million shares.
  • Sola reiterated, "the goal is to double Sanmina revenue in the next 2 years. And what we see today, the AI opportunities are on track to deliver a $16-plus billion in our calendar year '27."
  • Faust commented, "We continue to expect the core Sanmina business to grow high single digits this fiscal year."

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2026 was $3.19 billion, up 59% year-over-year.
  • Non-GAAP gross profit was $298 million or 9.3% of revenue, a 30 basis point improvement year-over-year.
  • Non-GAAP operating profit was $192 million or 6.0% of revenue, up 40 basis points year-over-year.
  • IMS revenue reached $2.79 billion, up 72% year-over-year, with a non-GAAP gross margin of 8.7%.
  • CPS revenue was $434 million, up 4.3% year-over-year, with a non-GAAP gross margin of 12.9%.
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at $1.42 billion, and free cash flow was $92 million.
  • Faust noted, "We continue to have one of the strongest balance sheets in the industry with a net leverage ratio of 0.8x."

Q&A

  • Ruplu Bhattacharya, BofA Securities: Asked about sequential revenue guidance and ZT's contribution. Sola replied, "our business is improving in the second quarter... the core business will grow quarter-over-quarter and will grow double digits year-over-year, okay? And we expect ZT system to grow quarter-over-quarter."
  • Bhattacharya followed up on ZT's transition from NVIDIA to AMD. Faust explained, "the opportunity... for accelerated compute specifically is huge. And that's what we're focused on right now and doing our part of that to be able to execute on that demand and on that opportunity."
  • Bhattacharya asked about the $14 billion revenue consensus for 2026. Sola answered, "we believe we have a very good chance of hitting $14 billion."
  • Steven Fox, Fox Advisors: Queried operating margin sustainability. Faust responded, "it's largely dependent on mix... we're always looking to drive operational efficiencies... growing those businesses that are accretive to the overall margin profile."
  • Fox asked about ZT wins for later in the year. Sola said, "why we're winning is execution. This is a great team. They are known to execute."
  • Anja Soderstrom, Sidoti & Company: Questioned industrial, medical, auto, and defense segment weakness. Sola cited, "some weakness in the last couple of quarters in automotive, transportation... they're starting to stabilize... Medical is starting to recover pretty nicely."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed an optimistic tone, seeking more details on growth trajectories, ZT Systems' integration, and market segment performance. Questions were probing but not adversarial, with keen interest in revenue targets and operational execution.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat sentiment, consistently reinforcing growth prospects and the successful ZT Systems integration. Sola's language, "I'm excited about the future opportunities," and "very comfortable, very excited with the [ZT] team," reflected high confidence. The tone remained positive in both prepared remarks and the Q&A, with no notable defensive or hesitant responses.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management exhibit increased confidence, with more direct discussion of ambitious revenue targets and integration outcomes.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language advanced from integrating ZT Systems to targeting $16 billion revenue in 2027, with management emphasizing AI-driven growth.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward vertical integration in AI data centers and expansion of energy business, including a new Houston facility.
  • Analysts' focus moved from acquisition integration risks to detailed inquiries about product mix, segment performance, and specific growth drivers.
  • Key metrics such as revenue, operating margin, and free cash flow all showed significant growth from the previous quarter, bolstered by the ZT Systems contribution.
  • Management's confidence level increased, with more explicit commitments to future growth and margin expansion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Sola acknowledged "material shortages... around memory and some of the special ASICs" in the communications segment but maintained strong demand outlook.
  • Inventory turns decreased to 5.3x, attributed to the ZT Systems acquisition, but Faust noted, "core Sanmina inventory turns, net of customer advances improved for the quarter."
  • Management emphasized the need for ongoing focus on working capital optimization, especially as ZT Systems is fully integrated in future quarters.
  • Market uncertainties from tariffs and geopolitical tensions were factored into guidance.

Final Takeaway

Sanmina management underscored a strong start to fiscal 2026, driven by robust revenue growth, sustained margin expansion, and the successful integration of ZT Systems. The company is positioning itself for significant AI and data center opportunity, targeting $16 billion in revenue by 2027, and maintaining a healthy balance sheet to support organic growth and strategic investments. Management remains confident in its ability to deliver on ambitious targets, supported by a diversified end-market presence and a disciplined capital allocation strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Runway Growth Finance announces notes offering
data.description:
• Runway Growth Finance (RWAY) commenced an underwritten offering of unsecured notes to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market.
  • If approved, the trading will potentially start within 30 days of issuance.
  • The interest rates and terms will be set at pricing.
  • Net proceeds will be used to repay outstanding debt—including up to $51.75 million of the company's 8.00% notes due December 2027—finance its prior SWK Holdings acquisition, and support general corporate purposes.

data.title: Tesla struggles in Europe as BYD surges past EV sales
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) finished 2025 with weaker European sales, as Chinese automaker BYD (BYDDF) continued to outperform Elon Musk’s EV company.

New-car registrations for Tesla models, a reflection of sales, fell 20% year-on-year to 35,280 units in December across the European Union, the U.K., Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland, according to the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association. On an annual basis, Tesla (TSLA) sales slipped 27% to 238,656 units.

By contrast, China’s BYD saw a remarkable 268.7% jump in European sales, reaching 187,657 units, while its market share rose to 1.4% from 0.4% in 2024.

Battery-electric cars accounted for 17.4% of the EU market share in 2025, an increase from the low baseline of 13.6% one year earlier.

Hybrid-electric car registrations captured 34.5% of the market, remaining the preferred choice among consumers in the EU.

Meanwhile, the combined market share of petrol and diesel cars fell to 35.5%, down from 45.2% in 2024.

The year-over-year variation for December 2025 showed a surge of 51% for battery-electric and 36.7% for plug-in-hybrid electric cars, while hybrid-electric recorded a 5.8% increase.

Source: Press release

data.title: Twin Hospitality Group files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
Salesforce unit secures $5.6B contract for U.S. Army
Amazon Web Services secures $581.3M U.S. Air Force contract
data.description:
• Amazon Web Services secured a ~$581.3M firm-fixed-price contract for the Cloud One Program. This contract provides for Amazon Cloud Service supporting the Air Force's Cloud One Program and their customers.
  • The work will be performed at the contractor's designated facilities across the contiguous U.S. and is expected to be completed by Dec. 7, 2028.
  • The fiscal 2026 operations and maintenance funds in the amount of ~$3.5M are being obligated at the time of award.
  • The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center, Massachusetts, is the contracting entity.

data.title: China's Anta Sports to buy 29% stake in Puma
data.description:
• China's Anta Sports (ANPDY) to acquire a 29.06% stake in Puma (PMMAF) from the French Pinault family's Artémis holding company for ~$1.8B, making it the largest shareholder of the German sportswear maker.
  • Anta (ANPDY) will purchase 43M Puma (PMMAF) shares at €35 each in an all-cash transaction funded by its internal cash reserves. The price represents a 62% premium to Puma's €21.63 closing price on Monday. The stock price jumped about 17% on the European exchange after the news.
  • The deal is expected to close by the end of 2026.
  • The Hong Kong-listed company explicitly rules out a full takeover, committing to Puma's independent governance while exploring future partnership enhancements.
  • "ANTA has always admired PUMA’s long-term brand value and potential. Such strong brand DNA and value heritage are rare to come by. We believe PUMA’s share price over the past few months does not fully reflect the long-term potential of the brand. We have confidence in its management team and strategic transformation." Ding Shizhong, board chairman of ANTA Sports, commented.

data.title: SK Hynix nears record high on reported Microsoft AI memory deal
data.description:
• SK Hynix Inc. (HXSC.F) shares surged toward a record closing high after local media reported the company is the exclusive supplier of advanced memory for Microsoft’s (MSFT) new AI chip.
  • The stock rose as much as 7.7% on the Korea Exchange, erasing an early loss on the latest tariff threat from US President Donald Trump.
  • The stock has kept rising this year, fueling an AI-driven rally that has pushed the South Korean company’s market value close to $400 billion.
